title: User can add a product to the cart from the
description: User can add a product to the cart from the product detail page and see the cart item count update
kane_summary:
The run attempted to use the site’s search box to find multiple products in sequence (first “iphone”, then “mac”, then “canon”).
It successfully entered “iphone” in the “Search For Products” field on the homepage and opened an iPhone product page (showing “iPhone” with an “Out Of Stock” label).
From the iPhone product page, it entered “mac” in the search field and clicked a “MacBook” result from the search suggestions, which navigated to a MacBook product page.
The run then tried to type “canon” into the search box while still on a product page, but the automation ended before it could complete the next action (for example, selecting a Canon result or loading a Canon product/search results page).
Why it likely failed: the flow appears incomplete—after typing “canon” there’s no recorded click on a search suggestion or the Search button, and no final page showing Canon results; the script may have stopped early or couldn’t find/activate the next expected search result element.

title: User can open the cart and see the list of
description: User can open the cart and see the list of added items with their names and prices
kane_one_liner: attempted to open the cart and select a product option on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
kane_summary:
The run navigated to the LambdaTest eCommerce Playground site and opened the cart panel from the top-right cart icon.
The cart slide-out showed “Your shopping cart is empty!” with $0.00 totals, indicating nothing was successfully added to the cart at that point.
It then opened a product page (URL shows product_id=30). The visible product page in the screenshots is for “Canon EOS 5D” and shows a required option dropdown (“--- Please Select ---” with a red “Size required!” message), suggesting the product needs an option selected before it can be added to the cart.
A later step attempted to click a specific product option element (DOM id #input-option230-216836), likely to satisfy the required “Size” selection.
The automation failed immediately after that with “step file not found,” so it never completed the next action (such as adding the item to the cart or proceeding to checkout). This looks like a test/script issue rather than a user-visible site error, and it prevented finishing the shopping flow.
There is also a mismatch between the intended product (“Microsoft smartwatch” was clicked earlier) and the product page shown (“Canon EOS 5D”), which likely indicates the flow navigated to an unexpected product page or the product selection step did not land on the correct item.

title: User can navigate to the product catalog and see a
description: User can navigate to the product catalog and see a list of products
kane_one_liner: opened the Laptops product catalog on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
kane_summary:
Opened https://ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io/.
Used the site navigation to go to the product browsing/catalog area.
Landed on the Laptops category page where a grid of product items is visible.

title: User can apply a brand filter from the sidebar to
description: User can apply a brand filter from the sidebar to narrow product results
kane_one_liner: attempted to filter Components by Apple on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
kane_summary:
The run navigated to the LambdaTest Ecommerce Playground site and clicked “SHOP NOW.”
It opened the “Shop by Category” menu and selected the “Components” category, landing on the Components listing page (URL shows category path=25).
On the Components page, the left-side FILTER panel shows a “MANUFACTURER” section with “Apple” listed as an option, and the automation attempted to click the “Apple” manufacturer filter.
The run failed before confirming any filtered results (there’s no evidence of the product list updating after the Apple click). This commonly happens when the filter click doesn’t take effect—e.g., the page didn’t finish loading, the filter list was partially off-screen/collapsed, or the click landed on a non-clickable area of the manufacturer row.
Because the objective and final URL were not recorded in the run output, the last reliable state is the Components category page where the Apple filter was about to be applied.

title: View product highlights without logging in

Finished on the homepage with browsing access confirmed.

title: User can search for a product by name and see
description: User can search for a product by name and see relevant results

A results dropdown appeared under the search bar showing multiple “iPhone” product entries with prices, confirming the search worked.
